turns.user_message: I used to just let the constant demands roll right off my shoulders without a second thought. Things that used to not bother me at all now make my chest tighten instantly. Back then, a heavy load felt like a normal part of the routine, and I could easily push through it. Now, even a minor request makes me want to shut everything down. I catch myself staring at the same screen for way too long, trying to muster up the energy to just hit reply. It is frustrating how quickly my patience has completely vanished.

I really wish I could go back to how I used to handle everything so effortlessly. A few months ago, I would just tackle the backlog and move on without dwelling on it. Lately, the same pile of tasks just sits there mocking me while I scroll aimlessly. I keep telling myself I will dive in tomorrow, but the dread just builds up overnight instead. It is exhausting to feel this heavy resistance every single morning when I know I should just get started.

I honestly do not recognize the person I used to be when I could power through anything without a second thought. Those days of bouncing back from a tough week feel like they belong to someone else entirely now. Every new notification sends a sharp wave of irritation straight through my chest, and I just want to close my eyes and pretend it is not there. I sit at my desk completely paralyzed while the hours slip away, hating how my own mind keeps shutting down on me. The sheer weight of it all has completely drained whatever spark I had left.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This used to not bother the speaker.
